message:
fix(web,oracle,h1,sync,time,grpc): post-handler cancel check, supervision causal windows, trailer validation, barrier/interval/health fixes

src/web/request_region.rs:
- Add post-handler cancel check in run() and run_sync(): if
  cx.is_cancel_requested() after the handler returns Ok or Err, return
  RegionOutcome::Cancelled instead, honoring the documented 499 semantics
- Add regression tests: run_cancelled_during_handler_returns_499 and
  run_sync_cancelled_during_handler_returns_499

src/lab/oracle/actor.rs:
- Remove restart_counts HashMap — the oracle now computes restart counts
  per failure from the restart log within the causal window rather than
  accumulating a global counter that leaked across incident boundaries
- Add next_failure_time() and restart_attempt_in_window() helpers for
  bounded causal-window evaluation
- Fix sibling-not-restarted check to use restarted_in_window() instead
  of scanning the entire restart log

src/http/h1/{client,stream}.rs:
- Add validate_header_field() call before appending parsed trailer headers
  to reject malformed trailer field names/values (e.g. embedded newlines)

src/sync/barrier.rs:
- Replace impossible fallback path (re-push waiter if slot not found in
  same generation) with unreachable!() — if the generation hasn't changed,
  the waiter must still be present

src/time/interval.rs:
- Replace implicit Copy/Clone tests with explicit assert_clone/assert_copy
  compile-time trait bound checks for MissedTickBehavior

src/grpc/health.rs:
- Fix watch() to atomically capture (status, version) in a single call
  to watched_status_and_version() instead of two separate calls that
  could race with a concurrent set_status()
- Add regression test: health_watch_initial_snapshot_is_atomic_for_named_services

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
